location: Koninginnenhoofd 1, Rotterdam, Netherlands (Wilhelminapier, Kop van Zuid)
architects: J. Muller & C.M. Droogleever Fortuyn (1901), expanded by J. Muller & C.B. van der Tak (1908, 1916, 1920)

key_features: Twin octagonal towers with green copper cupolas (north tower 1916, south tower 1917/1920)
Red brick facade with natural stone decorations and baseboards
38-meter clock tower with clock faces on all 4 sides
Wind vane shaped like a sailing ship on south tower
Weather vanes on all 4 sides of south tower
Four facade panels depicting male figures representing different continents
Roofline reminiscent of ocean liner funnels
color_palette: Natural stone (light/cream) decorative elements and baseboards
Dark wrought iron railings and staircases

architectural_drawings.description: Rotterdam City Archive likely holds original architectural plans by J. Muller and C.M. Droogleever Fortuyn
architectural_drawings.notes: The Stadsarchief Rotterdam maintains the Holland Amerika Lijn corporate archive. Search their digital collection for building plans ('bouwtekeningen') of the kantoorgebouw.

architectural_drawings.description: Rijksdienst voor het Cultureel Erfgoed (RCE) may hold measured drawings for rijksmonument 513872
architectural_drawings.notes: The national heritage service often commissions or collects architectural surveys of rijksmonumenten. Search their beeldbank for monument number 513872.
